persona.name: Mystery Symptom Maria
persona.gender: Primarily female (70/30 F/M for DTC health testing)
persona.description: Has been dealing with unexplained symptoms for months or years. Bloating, fatigue, skin issues, headaches — she's been to doctors who say 'everything looks normal' or 'try eliminating dairy.' She's frustrated, tired of guessing, and ready to pay for real answers. She's Googled her symptoms a hundred times and suspects food might be involved but doesn't know which foods.

demographics.education: College educated, researches her health online
demographics.lifestyle: Busy, health-aware but not health-obsessed. Has tried elimination diets halfheartedly. Willing to invest in answers

pain_points: Unexplained chronic symptoms (bloating, fatigue, skin, headaches, brain fog)
Doctors haven't been helpful ('everything looks normal', 'it's probably stress')
Tried eliminating random foods but didn't see clear results
Doesn't know if it's food allergies, sensitivities, or something else
Feels like she's 'going crazy' or symptoms are 'in her head'
Overwhelmed by conflicting health info online
Previous allergy tests (skin prick) didn't catch anything but symptoms persist
Worried about cost but more worried about continuing to feel terrible
what_converts_her: Someone who LOOKS like her describing the SAME symptoms and getting answers
The revelation that standard tests only check 1-2 pathways (missing delayed reactions)
Seeing a sample color-coded report (tangible, real, not vague)
The insurance price point ($25 + $90 feels very different from $648)
Knowing a real dietitian will explain results (not left alone with data)
Speed: results in 3-5 days (she's been waiting months/years, this feels fast)
Simplicity: finger prick at home, mail it back. No doctor appointment needed
Emotional validation: 'your symptoms are REAL, here's what's causing them'
objections: Is this legit? (→ CLIA certified, COLA accredited, 1000+ providers)
It's expensive (→ insurance covers most of it, $25 + $90 with insurance)
I've done allergy tests before that didn't help (→ those tested 1-2 pathways, this tests 4)
Can I do this at home? (→ yes, finger prick option, no clinic needed)
How long until I feel better? (→ results in 3-5 days, dietary changes show results in 2-4 weeks)
Will my doctor accept these results? (→ CLIA/COLA certified, same as any lab)
persona.content_consumption: Health content, symptom Googling, 'what I eat in a day', gut health content, chronic illness community. Stops scrolling for 'I finally found out why I...' content

ad_character_match.should_look_like: A woman who's been through it. Not sick-looking, but tired. Someone who could be your coworker, neighbor, friend. Real skin, real tiredness, but put-together enough that you trust her judgment. Mid-late 20s to early 40s
ad_character_match.should_NOT_look_like: Medical professional (unless testimonial), fitness influencer, visibly ill, too young (early 20s don't have this problem set yet), too polished
